manifest_notes: Postgres CDC sync failure involving interval column types.

issue.title: Source Postgres: CDC syncs failing on `interval` type columns

labels.name: team/db-dw-sources
labels.description: Backlog for Database and Data Warehouse Sources team

issue.body:
### Connector Name

source-postgres

### Connector Version

2.0.33

### What step the error happened?

During the sync

### Revelant information

We have several tables with `interval` type columns in Postgres that we are trying to sync to snowflake (destination-version 1.0.5).

These tables work when syncing postgres via the standard method, but when attempting to use replication the sync errors out.  Clarification: replication-method = `Standard` in the connector, it works, but when I do replication-method = `Logical Replication (CDC)`  that's when it fails.

Log attached
[airbyte_cdc_interval_error (1).txt](https://github.com/airbytehq/airbyte/files/11802461/airbyte_cdc_interval_error.1.txt)

comments.body: Interval is not a supported type today

At Airbyte, we seek to be clear about the project priorities and roadmap. This issue has not had any activity for 365 days, suggesting that it's not as critical as others. It's possible it has already been fixed. It is being marked as stale and will be closed in 20 days if there is no activity. To keep it open, please comment to let us know why it is important to you and if it is still reproducible on recent versions of Airbyte.

performed_via_github_app.name: octavia-bot
performed_via_github_app.description:
This App is used by the Airbyte team to run CI/CD workflows within GitHub.

By adding this app to your Airbyte fork, you allow Airbyte to trigger tests within your fork and post test results into to your PRs.
